blocks.en: Claude Certified Architect — Foundations Certification
blocks.sr: Claude Certified Architect — Foundations sertifikacija

blocks.en: Study Guide (Based on the Official Exam Guide)
blocks.sr: Vodič za učenje (zasnovan na zvaničnom vodiču za ispit)

blocks.en: Introduction
blocks.sr: Uvod

blocks.en: The **Claude Certified Architect — Foundations** certification confirms that a specialist can make sound trade-off decisions when implementing real-world Claude-based solutions. The exam assesses foundational knowledge of Claude Code, the Claude Agent SDK, the Claude API, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP)—the core technologies for building production applications with Claude.
blocks.sr: Sertifikacija **Claude Certified Architect — Foundations** potvrđuje da specijalista može da donosi utemeljene odluke o kompromisima pri implementaciji stvarnih rešenja zasnovanih na Claude-u. Ispit proverava temeljno znanje o Claude Code, Claude Agent SDK, Claude API i Model Context Protocol (MCP) — ključnim tehnologijama za izgradnju produkcionih aplikacija sa Claude-om.

blocks.en: The exam questions are based on realistic industry scenarios: building agentic systems for customer support, designing multi-agent research pipelines, integrating Claude Code into CI/CD, creating developer productivity tools, and extracting structured data from unstructured documents.
blocks.sr: Pitanja na ispitu zasnovana su na realističnim industrijskim scenarijima: izgradnja agentskih sistema za korisničku podršku, projektovanje istraživačkih pipeline-ova sa više agenata, integracija Claude Code-a u CI/CD, kreiranje alata za produktivnost programera i ekstrakcija strukturisanih podataka iz nestrukturisanih dokumenata.

blocks.en: Target Candidate
blocks.sr: Ciljni kandidat

blocks.en: The ideal candidate is a **solution architect** who designs and ships production applications with Claude. You should have at least 6 months of hands-on experience with:
blocks.sr: Idealan kandidat je **arhitekta rešenja** koji projektuje i isporučuje produkcione aplikacije sa Claude-om. Treba da imate najmanje 6 meseci praktičnog iskustva sa:

blocks.en: **Claude Agent SDK** — multi-agent orchestration, delegating to subagents, tool integration, lifecycle hooks
blocks.sr: **Claude Agent SDK** — orkestracija više agenata, delegiranje podagentima, integracija alata, hook-ovi životnog ciklusa

blocks.en: **Claude Code** — CLAUDE.md, MCP servers, Agent Skills, planning mode
blocks.sr: **Claude Code** — CLAUDE.md, MCP serveri, Agent Skills, režim planiranja

blocks.en: **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** — tools and resources for backend integration
blocks.sr: **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** — alati i resursi za integraciju sa bekendom

blocks.en: **Prompt engineering** — JSON schemas, few-shot examples, data extraction templates
blocks.sr: **Prompt engineering** — JSON šeme, few-shot primeri, šabloni za ekstrakciju podataka

blocks.en: **Context windows** — working with long documents, multi-agent context passing
blocks.sr: **Kontekstni prozori** — rad sa dugačkim dokumentima, prosleđivanje konteksta između više agenata

blocks.en: **CI/CD pipelines** — automated code review, test generation
blocks.sr: **CI/CD pipeline-ovi** — automatizovani pregled koda, generisanje testova

blocks.en: **Escalation and reliability** — error handling, human-in-the-loop
blocks.sr: **Eskalacija i pouzdanost** — rukovanje greškama, human-in-the-loop

blocks.en: Exam Format
blocks.sr: Format ispita

blocks.en: Exam Content: 5 Domains
blocks.sr: Sadržaj ispita: 5 domena

cells.sr: 3. Konfiguracija i radni tokovi Claude Code-a

blocks.en: Exam Scenarios
blocks.sr: Scenariji na ispitu

blocks.en: Scenario 1: Customer Support Agent
blocks.sr: Scenario 1: Agent za korisničku podršku

blocks.en: You build an agent to handle returns, billing disputes, and account issues using the Claude Agent SDK. The agent uses MCP tools (`get_customer`, `lookup_order`, `process_refund`, `escalate_to_human`). The target is 80%+ first-contact resolution with appropriate escalation.
blocks.sr: Gradite agenta koji obrađuje povraćaje robe, sporove oko naplate i probleme sa nalogom koristeći Claude Agent SDK. Agent koristi MCP alate (`get_customer`, `lookup_order`, `process_refund`, `escalate_to_human`). Cilj je 80%+ rešavanja pri prvom kontaktu uz odgovarajuću eskalaciju.

blocks.en: Scenario 2: Code Generation with Claude Code
blocks.sr: Scenario 2: Generisanje koda pomoću Claude Code-a

blocks.en: You use Claude Code to accelerate development: code generation, refactoring, debugging, documentation. You need to integrate it with custom slash commands and CLAUDE.md configuration, and understand when to use planning mode.
blocks.sr: Koristite Claude Code za ubrzanje razvoja: generisanje koda, refaktorisanje, debagovanje, dokumentaciju. Treba da ga integrišete sa prilagođenim slash komandama i CLAUDE.md konfiguracijom, kao i da razumete kada koristiti režim planiranja.

blocks.en: Scenario 3: Multi-Agent Research System
blocks.sr: Scenario 3: Istraživački sistem sa više agenata

blocks.en: A coordinator agent delegates tasks to specialized subagents: web research, document analysis, synthesis, and report generation. The system must produce complete reports with citations.
blocks.sr: Agent koordinator delegira zadatke specijalizovanim podagentima: istraživanje veba, analiza dokumenata, sinteza i generisanje izveštaja. Sistem mora da proizvodi potpune izveštaje sa citatima.

blocks.en: Scenario 4: Developer Productivity Tools
blocks.sr: Scenario 4: Alati za produktivnost programera

blocks.en: The agent helps engineers explore unfamiliar codebases, generate boilerplate code, and automate routine tasks. Built-in tools (Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob) and MCP servers are used.
blocks.sr: Agent pomaže inženjerima da istraže nepoznate baze koda, generišu boilerplate kod i automatizuju rutinske zadatke. Koriste se ugrađeni alati (Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob) i MCP serveri.

blocks.en: Scenario 5: Claude Code for Continuous Integration
blocks.sr: Scenario 5: Claude Code za kontinuiranu integraciju

blocks.en: Integrate Claude Code into a CI/CD pipeline for automated code reviews, test generation, and pull request feedback. Prompts must be designed to minimize false positives.
blocks.sr: Integrišite Claude Code u CI/CD pipeline za automatizovane preglede koda, generisanje testova i povratne informacije na pull request-ove. Promptovi moraju biti osmišljeni tako da svedu lažno pozitivne rezultate na minimum.

blocks.en: Scenario 6: Structured Data Extraction
blocks.sr: Scenario 6: Ekstrakcija strukturisanih podataka

blocks.en: The system extracts information from unstructured documents, validates output with JSON schemas, and maintains high accuracy. It must correctly handle edge cases.
blocks.sr: Sistem ekstrahuje informacije iz nestrukturisanih dokumenata, validira izlaz pomoću JSON šema i održava visoku tačnost. Mora ispravno da obrađuje granične slučajeve.

blocks.en: Scenario 7: Conversational AI Architecture Patterns
blocks.sr: Scenario 7: Arhitektonski obrasci konverzacione veštačke inteligencije

blocks.en: You design multi-turn conversational systems covering context window management, instruction persistence across turns, memory strategies, tool design for safe execution, and handling ambiguous or conflicting user inputs.
blocks.sr: Projektujete konverzacione sisteme sa više rundi koji pokrivaju upravljanje kontekstnim prozorom, postojanost instrukcija kroz runde, strategije memorije, dizajn alata za bezbedno izvršavanje i obradu dvosmislenih ili konfliktnih korisničkih unosa.

blocks.en: Scenario 8: Agentic AI Tools *(content missing — help us fill it in!)*
blocks.sr: Scenario 8: Agentski alati veštačke inteligencije *(sadržaj nedostaje — pomozite nam da ga popunimo!)*

blocks.en: This scenario has been reported by exam candidates but is not yet covered in this guide. If you have encountered questions from this scenario on the real exam, please share them in [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/paullarionov/claude-certified-architect/issues) so we can add full coverage. Your contribution will help everyone preparing for the exam.
blocks.sr: Ovaj scenario su prijavili kandidati na ispitu, ali još nije obrađen u ovom vodiču. Ako ste naišli na pitanja iz ovog scenarija na pravom ispitu, podelite ih u [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/paullarionov/claude-certified-architect/issues) kako bismo mogli da dodamo potpunu pokrivenost. Vaš doprinos će pomoći svima koji se pripremaju za ispit.

blocks.en: Official Documentation
blocks.sr: Zvanična dokumentacija

blocks.en: PART I: THEORY FOUNDATIONS
blocks.sr: DEO I: TEORIJSKE OSNOVE

blocks.en: This part covers all the theory you need to pass the exam successfully. The material is organized by technologies and concepts rather than by exam domains—this helps you build a deeper understanding of each topic.
blocks.sr: Ovaj deo pokriva svu teoriju koja vam je potrebna da uspešno položite ispit. Materijal je organizovan po tehnologijama i konceptima, a ne po ispitnim domenima — to vam pomaže da izgradite dublje razumevanje svake teme.

blocks.en: Chapter 1: Claude API — Fundamentals of Model Interaction
blocks.sr: Poglavlje 1: Claude API — Osnove interakcije sa modelom

blocks.en: Documentation: [Messages API](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/messages) | [Prompt Engineering](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/overview)
blocks.sr: Dokumentacija: [Messages API](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/api/messages) | [Prompt Engineering](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/overview)

blocks.en: 1.1 API Request Structure
blocks.sr: 1.1 Struktura API zahteva

blocks.en: The Claude API follows a request–response model. Each request to the Claude Messages API includes:
blocks.sr: Claude API funkcioniše po modelu zahtev–odgovor. Svaki zahtev ka Claude Messages API-ju uključuje:

blocks.en: **Key fields:**
blocks.sr: **Ključna polja:**

blocks.en: `model` — model selection (`claude-opus-4-6`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-haiku-4-5`)
blocks.sr: `model` — izbor modela (`claude-opus-4-6`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-haiku-4-5`)

blocks.en: `max_tokens` — maximum number of tokens in the response
blocks.sr: `max_tokens` — maksimalan broj tokena u odgovoru

blocks.en: `system` — the system prompt (defines model behavior)
blocks.sr: `system` — sistemski prompt (definiše ponašanje modela)

blocks.en: `messages` — conversation history (**you must send the full history** to maintain coherence)
blocks.sr: `messages` — istorija konverzacije (**morate poslati celu istoriju** da biste održali koherentnost)

blocks.en: `tools` — definitions of available tools
blocks.sr: `tools` — definicije dostupnih alata

blocks.en: `tool_choice` — tool selection strategy
blocks.sr: `tool_choice` — strategija izbora alata

blocks.en: 1.2 Message Roles
blocks.sr: 1.2 Uloge poruka

blocks.en: The `messages` array uses three roles:
blocks.sr: Niz `messages` koristi tri uloge:

blocks.en: `user` — user messages
blocks.sr: `user` — korisničke poruke

blocks.en: `assistant` — model responses (included when sending history)
blocks.sr: `assistant` — odgovori modela (uključuju se pri slanju istorije)

blocks.en: `tool` — tool call results (the role is not explicitly set; this appears as a `tool_result` content block)
blocks.sr: `tool` — rezultati poziva alata (uloga se ne postavlja eksplicitno; ovo se pojavljuje kao `tool_result` blok sadržaja)

blocks.en: **Critically important:** on every API request you must send the **full conversation history**. The model does not persist state between requests—each call is independent.
blocks.sr: **Kritično važno:** pri svakom API zahtevu morate poslati **celu istoriju konverzacije**. Model ne čuva stanje između zahteva — svaki poziv je nezavisan.

blocks.en: 1.3 The `stop_reason` Field in the Response
blocks.sr: 1.3 Polje `stop_reason` u odgovoru

blocks.en: The Claude API response includes `stop_reason`, which indicates why the model stopped generating:
blocks.sr: Odgovor Claude API-ja uključuje `stop_reason`, koji pokazuje zašto je model prestao da generiše:

cells.en: Show the result to the user
The model wants to call a tool
Execute the tool and return the result
The response is truncated; you may need to increase the limit
cells.sr: Odgovor je skraćen; možda ćete morati da povećate limit
Model je naišao na zaustavnu sekvencu

cells.en: Handle based on your application logic

blocks.en: For agentic systems, `"tool_use"` and `"end_turn"` are the most important—they control the agent loop.
blocks.sr: Za agentske sisteme, `"tool_use"` i `"end_turn"` su najvažniji — oni upravljaju agentskom petljom.

blocks.en: 1.4 System Prompt
blocks.sr: 1.4 Sistemski prompt

blocks.en: The system prompt is a special instruction that defines context and behavioral rules. It:
blocks.sr: Sistemski prompt je posebna instrukcija koja definiše kontekst i pravila ponašanja. On:

blocks.en: Is not part of the `messages` array; it is passed separately in the `system` field
blocks.sr: Nije deo niza `messages`; prosleđuje se odvojeno u polju `system`

blocks.en: Has priority over user messages
blocks.sr: Ima prioritet nad korisničkim porukama

blocks.en: Is loaded once and applies throughout the conversation
blocks.sr: Učitava se jednom i važi tokom cele konverzacije

blocks.en: Is used to define role, constraints, and output format
blocks.sr: Koristi se za definisanje uloge, ograničenja i formata izlaza

blocks.en: **Important for the exam:** system prompt wording can create unintended tool associations. For example, an instruction like “always verify the customer” can cause the model to overuse `get_customer`, even when it is unnecessary.
blocks.sr: **Važno za ispit:** formulacija sistemskog prompta može stvoriti neželjene asocijacije sa alatima. Na primer, instrukcija poput „uvek verifikuj korisnika“ može navesti model da preterano koristi `get_customer`, čak i kada to nije neophodno.

blocks.en: 1.5 Context Window
blocks.sr: 1.5 Kontekstni prozor

blocks.en: The context window is the total amount of text (in tokens) the model can process at once. It includes:
blocks.sr: Kontekstni prozor je ukupna količina teksta (u tokenima) koju model može da obradi odjednom. On uključuje:

blocks.en: The system prompt
blocks.sr: Sistemski prompt

blocks.en: The full message history
blocks.sr: Celu istoriju poruka

blocks.en: Tool definitions
blocks.sr: Definicije alata

blocks.en: Tool results
blocks.sr: Rezultate alata

blocks.en: **Key context-window problems:**
blocks.sr: **Ključni problemi kontekstnog prozora:**

blocks.en: **Lost-in-the-middle effect:** models reliably process information at the start and end of a long input but can miss details in the middle. Mitigation: place key information near the beginning or end.
blocks.sr: **Efekat gubljenja u sredini (lost-in-the-middle):** modeli pouzdano obrađuju informacije na početku i kraju dugog unosa, ali mogu propustiti detalje u sredini. Ublažavanje: postavite ključne informacije blizu početka ili kraja.

blocks.en: **Accumulation of tool results:** every tool call adds output to the context. If a tool returns 40+ fields but only 5 matter, then most of the context is wasted.
blocks.sr: **Nagomilavanje rezultata alata:** svaki poziv alata dodaje izlaz u kontekst. Ako alat vraća 40+ polja, a samo 5 je bitno, onda se većina konteksta troši uzalud.

blocks.en: **Progressive summarization:** when compressing history, numeric values, percentages, and dates often get lost and become vague (“about”, “roughly”, “a few”).
blocks.sr: **Progresivno sažimanje:** pri kompresovanju istorije, numeričke vrednosti, procenti i datumi se često gube i postaju neodređeni („otprilike“, „grubo“, „nekoliko“).

blocks.en: Chapter 2: Tools and `tool_use`
blocks.sr: Poglavlje 2: Alati i `tool_use`

blocks.en: Documentation: [Tool Use](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/tool-use)
blocks.sr: Dokumentacija: [Tool Use](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/tool-use)

blocks.en: 2.1 What is `tool_use`
blocks.sr: 2.1 Šta je `tool_use`

blocks.en: `tool_use` is a mechanism that allows Claude to call external functions. The model does not run code directly—it generates a structured tool call request; your code executes it and returns the result.
blocks.sr: `tool_use` je mehanizam koji omogućava Claude-u da poziva eksterne funkcije. Model ne izvršava kod direktno — on generiše strukturiran zahtev za poziv alata; vaš kod ga izvršava i vraća rezultat.

blocks.en: 2.2 Tool Definition
blocks.sr: 2.2 Definicija alata

blocks.en: Each tool is defined using a JSON schema:
blocks.sr: Svaki alat se definiše pomoću JSON šeme:

blocks.en: **Critically important aspects of a tool description:**
blocks.sr: **Kritično važni aspekti opisa alata:**

blocks.en: **The description is the primary selection mechanism.** An LLM chooses tools based on their descriptions. Minimal descriptions (“Retrieves customer information”) lead to mistakes when tools overlap.
blocks.sr: **Opis je primarni mehanizam za izbor.** LLM bira alate na osnovu njihovih opisa. Minimalni opisi („Dobavlja informacije o klijentu”) dovode do grešaka kada se alati preklapaju.

blocks.en: **Include in the description:**
blocks.sr: **U opis uključite:**

blocks.en: What the tool does and returns
blocks.sr: Šta alat radi i šta vraća

blocks.en: Input formats and example values
blocks.sr: Formate ulaza i primere vrednosti

blocks.en: Edge cases and constraints
blocks.sr: Granične slučajeve i ograničenja

blocks.en: When to use this tool vs similar alternatives
blocks.sr: Kada koristiti ovaj alat naspram sličnih alternativa

blocks.en: **Avoid** identical or overlapping descriptions across tools. If `analyze_content` and `analyze_document` have nearly identical descriptions, the model will confuse them.
blocks.sr: **Izbegavajte** identične ili preklapajuće opise među alatima. Ako `analyze_content` i `analyze_document` imaju gotovo identične opise, model će ih pomešati.

blocks.en: **Built-in tools vs MCP tools:** agents may prefer built-in tools (Read, Grep) over MCP tools with similar functionality. To prevent this, strengthen MCP tool descriptions—highlight concrete advantages, unique data, or context that built-in tools cannot provide.
blocks.sr: **Ugrađeni alati naspram MCP alata:** agenti mogu davati prednost ugrađenim alatima (Read, Grep) u odnosu na MCP alate sa sličnom funkcionalnošću. Da biste to sprečili, ojačajte opise MCP alata — istaknite konkretne prednosti, jedinstvene podatke ili kontekst koji ugrađeni alati ne mogu da pruže.

blocks.en: 2.3 The `tool_choice` Parameter
blocks.sr: 2.3 Parametar `tool_choice`

blocks.en: `tool_choice` controls how the model selects tools:
blocks.sr: `tool_choice` kontroliše kako model bira alate:

cells.en: The model decides whether to call a tool or answer in text
cells.sr: Model odlučuje da li će pozvati alat ili odgovoriti tekstom

cells.en: When you need guaranteed structured output
cells.sr: Kada vam je potreban zagarantovan strukturiran izlaz

cells.en: The model **must** call a specific tool
When you need a forced first step / execution order
cells.sr: Kada vam je potreban forsiran prvi korak / redosled izvršavanja

blocks.en: **Important scenarios:**
blocks.sr: **Važni scenariji:**

blocks.en: `tool_choice: "any"` + multiple extraction tools → the model picks the best one, but you still get structured output
blocks.sr: `tool_choice: "any"` + više alata za ekstrakciju → model bira najbolji, ali i dalje dobijate strukturiran izlaz

blocks.en: Forced selection → when you must guarantee a specific first action (e.g., `extract_metadata` before enrichment)
blocks.sr: Forsiran izbor → kada morate da garantujete određenu prvu akciju (npr. `extract_metadata` pre obogaćivanja)

blocks.en: 2.4 JSON Schemas for Structured Output
blocks.sr: 2.4 JSON šeme za strukturiran izlaz

blocks.en: Using `tool_use` with JSON schemas is the **most reliable** way to obtain structured output from Claude. It:
blocks.sr: Korišćenje `tool_use` sa JSON šemama je **najpouzdaniji** način da se od Claude-a dobije strukturiran izlaz. Ovaj pristup:

blocks.en: Guarantees syntactically valid JSON (no missing braces, no trailing commas)
blocks.sr: Garantuje sintaksno ispravan JSON (bez nedostajućih zagrada, bez zareza na kraju)

blocks.en: Enforces the required structure (required fields are present)
blocks.sr: Nameće obaveznu strukturu (obavezna polja su prisutna)

blocks.en: Does **not** guarantee semantic correctness (values can still be wrong)
blocks.sr: **Ne** garantuje semantičku ispravnost (vrednosti i dalje mogu biti pogrešne)

blocks.en: **Schema design — key principles:**
blocks.sr: **Dizajn šeme — ključni principi:**

blocks.en: **Schema design rules:**
blocks.sr: **Pravila za dizajn šeme:**

blocks.en: **Required vs optional:** mark fields as required only if the information is always available. Required fields push the model to fabricate values when data is missing.
blocks.sr: **Obavezna naspram opcionih:** označavajte polja kao obavezna samo ako je informacija uvek dostupna. Obavezna polja teraju model da izmišlja vrednosti kada podaci nedostaju.

blocks.en: **Nullable fields:** use `"type": ["string", "null"]` for information that may be absent. The model can return `null` instead of hallucinating.
blocks.sr: **Nullable polja:** koristite `"type": ["string", "null"]` za informacije koje mogu izostati. Model može da vrati `null` umesto da halucinira.

blocks.en: **Enums with `"other"`:** add `"other"` + a detail string to avoid losing data outside your predefined categories.
blocks.sr: **Enumi sa `"other"`:** dodajte `"other"` + string sa detaljima da ne biste izgubili podatke izvan svojih unapred definisanih kategorija.

blocks.en: **Enum `"unclear"`:** for cases where the model cannot confidently pick a category—honest `"unclear"` is better than a wrong category.
blocks.sr: **Enum `"unclear"`:** za slučajeve u kojima model ne može pouzdano da izabere kategoriju — pošteno `"unclear"` je bolje od pogrešne kategorije.

blocks.en: 2.5 Syntax vs Semantic Errors
blocks.sr: 2.5 Sintaksne naspram semantičkih grešaka

cells.en: Totals don't add up, value in wrong field, hallucination
cells.sr: Zbirovi se ne poklapaju, vrednost u pogrešnom polju, halucinacija

cells.en: Validation checks, retry with feedback, self-correction
cells.sr: Provere validacije, ponovni pokušaj uz povratnu informaciju, samokorekcija

blocks.en: Chapter 3: Claude Agent SDK — Building Agentic Systems
blocks.sr: Poglavlje 3: Claude Agent SDK — izgradnja agentskih sistema

blocks.en: Documentation: [Agent SDK](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/overview) | [Hooks](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/hooks) | [Subagents](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/subagents) | [Sessions](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/sessions)
blocks.sr: Dokumentacija: [Agent SDK](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/overview) | [Hooks](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/hooks) | [Subagents](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/subagents) | [Sessions](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/sessions)

blocks.en: 3.1 What is an Agentic Loop
blocks.sr: 3.1 Šta je agentska petlja

blocks.en: The agentic loop is the core pattern for autonomous task execution. The model doesn't just answer—it performs a sequence of actions:
blocks.sr: Agentska petlja je osnovni obrazac za autonomno izvršavanje zadataka. Model ne samo da odgovara — on izvodi niz akcija:

blocks.text:
1. Send a request to Claude with tools
2. Receive a response
3. Check stop_reason:
   - "tool_use" -> execute the tool, append the result to history, go back to step 1
   - "end_turn" -> the task is complete, show the result to the user
4. Repeat until completion

blocks.en: **This is a model-driven approach:** Claude decides which tool to call next based on context and prior tool results. This differs from hard-coded decision trees where the action sequence is fixed.
blocks.sr: **Ovo je pristup vođen modelom:** Claude na osnovu konteksta i prethodnih rezultata alata odlučuje koji će alat sledeći pozvati. To se razlikuje od čvrsto kodiranih stabala odlučivanja, gde je redosled akcija fiksan.

blocks.en: **Anti-patterns (avoid):**
blocks.sr: **Antiobrasci (izbegavaj):**

blocks.en: Parsing assistant text to detect completion (“Task completed”)
blocks.sr: Parsiranje teksta asistenta da bi se detektovalo dovršenje („Task completed“)

blocks.en: Using an arbitrary iteration limit (e.g., `max_iterations=5`) as the primary stop condition
blocks.sr: Korišćenje proizvoljnog ograničenja iteracija (npr. `max_iterations=5`) kao primarnog uslova zaustavljanja

blocks.en: Checking whether the assistant produced textual content as a completion signal
blocks.sr: Provera da li je asistent proizveo tekstualni sadržaj kao signal dovršenja

blocks.en: **Correct approach:** the only reliable completion signal is `stop_reason == "end_turn"`.
blocks.sr: **Ispravan pristup:** jedini pouzdan signal dovršenja je `stop_reason == "end_turn"`.

blocks.en: 3.2 `AgentDefinition` Configuration
blocks.sr: 3.2 Konfiguracija `AgentDefinition`

blocks.en: `AgentDefinition` is the agent configuration object in the Claude Agent SDK:
blocks.sr: `AgentDefinition` je objekat konfiguracije agenta u Claude Agent SDK-u:

blocks.text:
agent = AgentDefinition(
    name="customer_support",
    description="Handles customer requests for returns and order issues",
    system_prompt="You are a customer support agent...",
    allowed_tools=["get_customer", "lookup_order", "process_refund", "escalate_to_human"],
    # For a coordinator:
    # allowed_tools=["Task", "get_customer", ...]
)

blocks.en: **Key parameters:**
blocks.sr: **Ključni parametri:**

blocks.en: `name` / `description` — identification and description of the agent
blocks.sr: `name` / `description` — identifikacija i opis agenta

blocks.en: `system_prompt` — system prompt with instructions
blocks.sr: `system_prompt` — sistemski prompt sa instrukcijama

blocks.en: `allowed_tools` — list of allowed tools (principle of least privilege)
blocks.sr: `allowed_tools` — lista dozvoljenih alata (princip najmanje privilegije)

blocks.en: 3.3 Hub-and-Spoke: Coordinator and Subagents
blocks.sr: 3.3 Hub-and-Spoke: koordinator i podagenti

blocks.en: A multi-agent architecture is typically built as a hub-and-spoke topology:
blocks.sr: Višeagentska arhitektura se obično gradi kao hub-and-spoke topologija:

blocks.text:
Coordinator
        /     |      \
   Subagent1  Subagent2  Subagent3
    (search)   (analysis)   (synthesis)

blocks.en: **The coordinator is responsible for:**
blocks.sr: **Koordinator je odgovoran za:**

blocks.en: Decomposing the task into subtasks
blocks.sr: Razlaganje zadatka na podzadatke

blocks.en: Deciding which subagents are needed (dynamic selection)
blocks.sr: Odlučivanje koji su podagenti potrebni (dinamička selekcija)

blocks.en: Delegating work to subagents
blocks.sr: Delegiranje posla podagentima

blocks.en: Aggregating and validating results
blocks.sr: Agregiranje i validaciju rezultata

blocks.en: Handling errors and retries
blocks.sr: Rukovanje greškama i ponovnim pokušajima

blocks.en: Communicating results to the user
blocks.sr: Saopštavanje rezultata korisniku

blocks.en: **Critical principle: subagents have isolated context.**
blocks.sr: **Kritičan princip: podagenti imaju izolovan kontekst.**

blocks.en: Subagents do **not** automatically inherit the coordinator's conversation history
blocks.sr: Podagenti **ne** nasleđuju automatski istoriju konverzacije koordinatora

blocks.en: All required context must be **explicitly passed** in the subagent prompt
blocks.sr: Sav potreban kontekst mora biti **eksplicitno prosleđen** u promptu podagenta

blocks.en: Subagents do not share memory across calls
blocks.sr: Podagenti ne dele memoriju između poziva

blocks.en: All communication flows through the coordinator (for observability and error control)
blocks.sr: Sva komunikacija teče kroz koordinatora (radi osmotrivosti i kontrole grešaka)

blocks.en: 3.4 The `Task` Tool for Spawning Subagents
blocks.sr: 3.4 Alat `Task` za pokretanje podagenata

blocks.en: Subagents are spawned via the `Task` tool:
blocks.sr: Podagenti se pokreću preko alata `Task`:

blocks.text:
# The coordinator's allowedTools must include "Task"
coordinator_agent = AgentDefinition(
    allowed_tools=["Task", "get_customer"]
)

blocks.en: **Explicit context passing is mandatory:**
blocks.sr: **Eksplicitno prosleđivanje konteksta je obavezno:**

blocks.text:
# Bad: the subagent has no context
Task: "Analyze the document"

# Good: full context in the prompt
Task: "Analyze the following document.
Document: [full document text]
Prior search results: [web search results]
Output format requirements: [schema]"

blocks.en: **Parallel spawning:** a coordinator can call multiple `Task`s in one response—subagents run in parallel:
blocks.sr: **Paralelno pokretanje:** koordinator može pozvati više `Task`-ova u jednom odgovoru — podagenti se izvršavaju paralelno:

blocks.text:
# One coordinator response contains:
Task 1: "Search for articles about X"
Task 2: "Analyze document Y"
Task 3: "Search for articles about Z"
# All three run concurrently

blocks.en: 3.5 Hooks in the Agent SDK
blocks.sr: 3.5 Hook-ovi u Agent SDK-u

blocks.en: Hooks allow interception and transformation at specific points in the agent lifecycle.
blocks.sr: Hook-ovi omogućavaju presretanje i transformaciju na određenim tačkama u životnom ciklusu agenta.

blocks.en: **PostToolUse** intercepts a tool result before it is provided to the model:
blocks.sr: **PostToolUse** presreće rezultat alata pre nego što se prosledi modelu:

blocks.en: **Outgoing-call interception hook** blocks actions that violate policy:
blocks.sr: **Hook za presretanje odlaznih poziva** blokira akcije koje krše politiku:

blocks.en: **Key difference: hooks vs prompt instructions**
blocks.sr: **Ključna razlika: hook-ovi naspram instrukcija u promptu**

cells.en: Critical business rules, financial operations, compliance
cells.sr: Kritična poslovna pravila, finansijske operacije, usklađenost

blocks.en: **Rule:** when failure has financial, legal, or safety consequences—use hooks, not prompts.
blocks.sr: **Pravilo:** kada otkaz ima finansijske, pravne ili bezbednosne posledice — koristi hook-ove, ne promptove.

blocks.en: Chapter 4: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
blocks.sr: Poglavlje 4: Model Context Protocol (MCP)

blocks.en: Documentation: [MCP](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) | [Tools](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/concepts/tools) | [Resources](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/concepts/resources) | [Servers](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/concepts/servers)
blocks.sr: Dokumentacija: [MCP](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) | [Tools](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/concepts/tools) | [Resources](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/concepts/resources) | [Servers](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/concepts/servers)

blocks.en: 4.1 What is MCP
blocks.sr: 4.1 Šta je MCP

blocks.en: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open protocol for connecting external systems to Claude. MCP defines three primary resource types:
blocks.sr: Model Context Protocol (MCP) je otvoreni protokol za povezivanje eksternih sistema sa Claude-om. MCP definiše tri primarna tipa resursa:

blocks.en: **Tools** — functions the agent can call to perform actions (CRUD operations, API calls, command execution)
blocks.sr: **Tools** — funkcije koje agent može da pozove kako bi izvršio akcije (CRUD operacije, API pozivi, izvršavanje komandi)

blocks.en: **Resources** — data the agent can read for context (documentation, database schemas, content catalogs)
blocks.sr: **Resources** — podaci koje agent može da čita radi konteksta (dokumentacija, šeme baza podataka, katalozi sadržaja)

blocks.en: **Prompts** — predefined prompt templates for common tasks
blocks.sr: **Prompts** — unapred definisani šabloni promptova za uobičajene zadatke

blocks.en: 4.2 MCP Servers
blocks.sr: 4.2 MCP serveri

blocks.en: An MCP server is a process that implements the MCP protocol and provides tools/resources. When you connect to an MCP server:
blocks.sr: MCP server je proces koji implementira MCP protokol i pruža alate/resurse. Kada se povežete na MCP server:

blocks.en: All tools are discovered automatically
blocks.sr: Svi alati se otkrivaju automatski

blocks.en: Tools from all connected servers are available at once
blocks.sr: Alati sa svih povezanih servera dostupni su odjednom

blocks.en: Tool descriptions determine how the model will use them
blocks.sr: Opisi alata određuju kako će ih model koristiti

blocks.en: 4.3 Configuring MCP Servers
blocks.sr: 4.3 Konfigurisanje MCP servera

blocks.en: **Project configuration (`.mcp.json`)** — for team usage:
blocks.sr: **Konfiguracija na nivou projekta (`.mcp.json`)** — za timsku upotrebu:

blocks.en: **Key points:**
blocks.sr: **Ključne tačke:**

blocks.en: `.mcp.json` is stored at the project root and managed in version control
blocks.sr: `.mcp.json` se čuva u korenu projekta i njime se upravlja preko sistema za kontrolu verzija

blocks.en: Environment variables (`${GITHUB_TOKEN}`) are used for secrets—tokens themselves are not committed
blocks.sr: Promenljive okruženja (`${GITHUB_TOKEN}`) se koriste za tajne — sami tokeni se ne komituju

blocks.en: Available to all project contributors
blocks.sr: Dostupno svim saradnicima na projektu

blocks.en: **User configuration (`~/.claude.json`)** — for personal/experimental servers:
blocks.sr: **Korisnička konfiguracija (`~/.claude.json`)** — za lične/eksperimentalne servere:

blocks.en: Stored in the user's home directory
blocks.sr: Čuva se u korisnikovom home direktorijumu

blocks.en: Not shared via version control
blocks.sr: Ne deli se preko sistema za kontrolu verzija

blocks.en: Suitable for personal experiments and testing
blocks.sr: Pogodno za lične eksperimente i testiranje

blocks.en: **Choosing servers:**
blocks.sr: **Biranje servera:**

blocks.en: For standard integrations (Jira, GitHub, Slack), prefer existing community MCP servers
blocks.sr: Za standardne integracije (Jira, GitHub, Slack), prednost dajte postojećim MCP serverima iz zajednice

blocks.en: Build your own servers only for unique, team-specific workflows
blocks.sr: Sopstvene servere gradite samo za jedinstvene radne tokove specifične za tim

blocks.en: 4.4 The `isError` Flag in MCP
blocks.sr: 4.4 Zastavica `isError` u MCP-u

blocks.en: When an MCP tool encounters an error, it uses `isError: true` in the response. This signals to the agent that the call failed.
blocks.sr: Kada MCP alat naiđe na grešku, u odgovoru koristi `isError: true`. To signalizira agentu da poziv nije uspeo.

blocks.en: **Structured error (good):**
blocks.sr: **Strukturirana greška (dobro):**

blocks.en: **Generic error (anti-pattern):**
blocks.sr: **Generička greška (anti-obrazac):**

blocks.en: A generic error gives the agent no information for decision-making—should it retry, change the query, or escalate?
blocks.sr: Generička greška ne daje agentu nikakvu informaciju za donošenje odluke — da li da pokuša ponovo, promeni upit ili eskalira?

blocks.en: 4.5 MCP Resources
blocks.sr: 4.5 MCP resursi

blocks.en: Resources are data that an agent can request to get context without taking actions:
blocks.sr: Resursi su podaci koje agent može da zatraži kako bi dobio kontekst bez preduzimanja akcija:

blocks.en: Content catalogs (e.g., a list of all project tasks, hierarchical navigation)
blocks.sr: Katalozi sadržaja (npr. lista svih projektnih zadataka, hijerarhijska navigacija)

blocks.en: Database schemas (understanding data structure)
blocks.sr: Šeme baza podataka (razumevanje strukture podataka)

blocks.en: Documentation (API references, internal guides)
blocks.sr: Dokumentacija (API reference, interni vodiči)

blocks.en: Issue/task summaries
blocks.sr: Sažeci problema/zadataka

blocks.en: **Resource advantage:** the agent does not need exploratory tool calls to understand what data exists. A resource provides an immediate “map.”
blocks.sr: **Prednost resursa:** agent ne mora da pravi istraživačke pozive alata da bi razumeo koji podaci postoje. Resurs odmah pruža „mapu“.

blocks.en: Chapter 5: Claude Code — Configuration and Workflows
blocks.sr: Poglavlje 5: Claude Code — konfiguracija i radni tokovi

blocks.en: Documentation: [Claude Code](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview) | [Memory / CLAUDE.md](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory) | [Skills](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills) | [MCP](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp) | [Hooks](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) | [Sub-agents](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents) | [GitHub Actions](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/github-actions) | [Headless](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/headless)
blocks.sr: Dokumentacija: [Claude Code](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview) | [Memory / CLAUDE.md](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory) | [Skills](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills) | [MCP](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp) | [Hooks](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) | [Sub-agents](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents) | [GitHub Actions](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/github-actions) | [Headless](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/headless)

blocks.en: 5.1 The CLAUDE.md Hierarchy
blocks.sr: 5.1 Hijerarhija CLAUDE.md

blocks.en: CLAUDE.md is the instruction file(s) for Claude Code. There is a three-level hierarchy:
blocks.sr: CLAUDE.md je fajl (ili fajlovi) sa instrukcijama za Claude Code. Postoji troslojna hijerarhija:

blocks.text:
1. User-level: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
   - Applies only to that user
   - NOT shared via VCS
   - Personal preferences and working style

2. Project-level: .claude/CLAUDE.md or a root CLAUDE.md
   - Applies to all project contributors
   - Managed via VCS
   - Coding standards, testing standards, architectural decisions

3. Directory-level: CLAUDE.md in subdirectories
   - Applies when working with files in that directory
   - Conventions specific to that part of the codebase

blocks.en: **Common mistake:** a new team member does not receive project instructions because they were placed in `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (user-level) instead of `.claude/CLAUDE.md` (project-level).
blocks.sr: **Česta greška:** novi član tima ne dobija projektne instrukcije jer su one stavljene u `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (korisnički nivo) umesto u `.claude/CLAUDE.md` (projektni nivo).

blocks.en: 5.2 `@path` Syntax (File Imports)
blocks.sr: 5.2 `@path` sintaksa (uvoz fajlova)

blocks.en: CLAUDE.md can reference external files using `@path`, making configuration modular:
blocks.sr: CLAUDE.md može da referencira spoljne fajlove pomoću `@path`, čime konfiguracija postaje modularna:

blocks.text:
# Project CLAUDE.md

Coding standards are described in @./standards/coding-style.md
Test requirements are in @./standards/testing-requirements.md
Project overview is in @README.md and dependencies are in @package.json

blocks.en: **Rules for `@path`:**
blocks.sr: **Pravila za `@path`:**

blocks.en: Use `@` immediately before the file path (no space)
blocks.sr: Stavi `@` neposredno ispred putanje fajla (bez razmaka)

blocks.en: Relative and absolute paths are supported
blocks.sr: Podržane su i relativne i apsolutne putanje

blocks.en: Relative paths are resolved relative to the file that contains the import
blocks.sr: Relativne putanje se razrešavaju u odnosu na fajl koji sadrži uvoz

blocks.en: Maximum import nesting depth is 5
blocks.sr: Maksimalna dubina ugnežđenja uvoza je 5

blocks.en: This avoids duplication and lets each package include only relevant standards.
blocks.sr: Time se izbegava dupliranje i svaki paket može da uključi samo relevantne standarde.

blocks.en: 5.3 The `.claude/rules/` Directory
blocks.sr: 5.3 Direktorijum `.claude/rules/`

blocks.en: `.claude/rules/` is an alternative to a monolithic CLAUDE.md, used to organize rules by topic:
blocks.sr: `.claude/rules/` je alternativa monolitnom CLAUDE.md, koja se koristi za organizovanje pravila po temama:

blocks.text:
.claude/rules/
  testing.md          -- testing conventions
  api-conventions.md  -- API conventions
  deployment.md       -- deployment rules
  react-patterns.md   -- React patterns

blocks.en: **Key feature: YAML frontmatter with `paths` for conditional loading:**
blocks.sr: **Ključna mogućnost: YAML frontmatter sa `paths` za uslovno učitavanje:**

blocks.text:
---
paths: ["src/api/**/*"]
---

For API files, use async/await with explicit error handling.
Each endpoint must return a standard response wrapper.

---
paths: ["**/*.test.tsx", "**/*.test.ts"]
---

Tests must use describe/it blocks.
Use data factories instead of hardcoding.
Do not mock the database—use a test database.

blocks.en: **How it works:**
blocks.sr: **Kako radi:**

blocks.en: A rule is loaded **only** when Claude Code edits a file matching the `paths` pattern
blocks.sr: Pravilo se učitava **samo** kada Claude Code menja fajl koji odgovara `paths` obrascu

blocks.en: This saves context and tokens—irrelevant rules are not loaded
blocks.sr: Ovo štedi kontekst i tokene — irelevantna pravila se ne učitavaju

blocks.en: Glob patterns let you apply conventions by file type regardless of location (ideal for tests scattered across the codebase)
blocks.sr: Glob obrasci omogućavaju primenu konvencija po tipu fajla bez obzira na lokaciju (idealno za testove razbacane po kodu)

blocks.en: **When to use `.claude/rules/` with `paths` vs directory-level CLAUDE.md:**
blocks.sr: **Kada koristiti `.claude/rules/` sa `paths` naspram CLAUDE.md na nivou direktorijuma:**

blocks.en: `.claude/rules/` with `paths` — when conventions apply to files spread across many directories (tests, migrations)
blocks.sr: `.claude/rules/` sa `paths` — kada se konvencije odnose na fajlove razbacane po mnogim direktorijumima (testovi, migracije)

blocks.en: Directory-level CLAUDE.md — when conventions are tied to a specific directory and are not needed elsewhere
blocks.sr: CLAUDE.md na nivou direktorijuma — kada su konvencije vezane za određeni direktorijum i nisu potrebne drugde

blocks.en: 5.4 Custom Slash Commands and Skills
blocks.sr: 5.4 Prilagođene slash komande i veštine (Skills)

blocks.en: **Note:** in the current Claude Code version, custom commands (`.claude/commands/`) are unified with skills (`.claude/skills/`). Both formats create `/name` commands. The exam guide references `.claude/commands/`—that format is still supported.
blocks.sr: **Napomena:** u trenutnoj verziji Claude Code-a, prilagođene komande (`.claude/commands/`) objedinjene su sa veštinama (`.claude/skills/`). Oba formata kreiraju `/name` komande. Exam guide referencira `.claude/commands/` — taj format je i dalje podržan.

blocks.en: Slash commands are reusable prompt templates invoked via `/name`:
blocks.sr: Slash komande su prompt šabloni za višekratnu upotrebu koji se pozivaju preko `/name`:

blocks.en: **`.claude/commands/` format (legacy, supported):**
blocks.sr: **`.claude/commands/` format (nasleđeni, podržan):**

blocks.text:
.claude/commands/
  review.md        -- /review -- standard code review
  test-gen.md      -- /test-gen -- test generation

blocks.en: **`.claude/skills/` format (current):**
blocks.sr: **`.claude/skills/` format (aktuelni):**

blocks.text:
.claude/skills/
  review/SKILL.md  -- /review -- with frontmatter configuration
  test-gen/SKILL.md

blocks.en: **Project commands** (`.claude/commands/` or `.claude/skills/`):
blocks.sr: **Projektne komande** (`.claude/commands/` ili `.claude/skills/`):

blocks.en: Stored in VCS and available to everyone when cloning the repo
blocks.sr: Čuvaju se u VCS-u i dostupne su svima pri kloniranju repozitorijuma

blocks.en: Ensure consistent workflows across the team
blocks.sr: Obezbeđuju konzistentne radne tokove u celom timu

blocks.en: **User commands** (`~/.claude/commands/` or `~/.claude/skills/`):
blocks.sr: **Korisničke komande** (`~/.claude/commands/` ili `~/.claude/skills/`):

blocks.en: Personal commands not shared via VCS
blocks.sr: Lične komande koje se ne dele preko VCS-a

blocks.en: For individual workflows
blocks.sr: Za individualne radne tokove

blocks.en: 5.5 Skills — `.claude/skills/`
blocks.sr: 5.5 Veštine (Skills) — `.claude/skills/`

blocks.en: Skills are advanced commands configured via SKILL.md frontmatter:
blocks.sr: Veštine su napredne komande koje se konfigurišu preko SKILL.md frontmatter-a:

blocks.text:
---
context: fork
allowed-tools: ["Read", "Grep", "Glob"]
argument-hint: "Path to the directory to analyze"
---

Analyze the code structure in the specified directory.
Output a report on dependencies and architectural patterns.

blocks.en: **Frontmatter parameters:**
blocks.sr: **Frontmatter parametri:**

cells.en: Runs the skill in an isolated subagent. Verbose output does not pollute the main session
cells.sr: Pokreće veštinu u izolovanom podagentu. Opširan izlaz ne zagađuje glavnu sesiju

cells.en: Restricts which tools are available (security—e.g., the skill cannot delete files if not allowed)
cells.sr: Ograničava koji su alati dostupni (bezbednost — npr. veština ne može da briše fajlove ako to nije dozvoljeno)

cells.en: Hint that asks for an argument when invoked without parameters
cells.sr: Nagoveštaj koji traži argument kada se pozove bez parametara

blocks.en: **When to use a skill vs CLAUDE.md:**
blocks.sr: **Kada koristiti veštinu naspram CLAUDE.md:**

blocks.en: **Skill** — on-demand invocation for a specific task (review, analysis, generation)
blocks.sr: **Veština** — poziv na zahtev za konkretan zadatak (pregled, analiza, generisanje)

blocks.en: **CLAUDE.md** — always-loaded general standards and conventions
blocks.sr: **CLAUDE.md** — uvek učitani opšti standardi i konvencije

blocks.en: **Personal skills (`~/.claude/skills/`):**
blocks.sr: **Lične veštine (`~/.claude/skills/`):**

blocks.en: Create personal variants under different names so you don't affect teammates
blocks.sr: Kreiraj lične varijante pod drugim imenima da ne bi uticao na kolege iz tima

blocks.en: 5.6 Planning Mode vs Direct Execution
blocks.sr: 5.6 Planski režim naspram direktnog izvršavanja

blocks.en: **Planning mode:**
blocks.sr: **Planski režim:**

blocks.en: The model only investigates and plans; it does not make changes
blocks.sr: Model samo istražuje i planira; ne pravi izmene

blocks.en: Uses Read, Grep, Glob to explore the codebase
blocks.sr: Koristi Read, Grep, Glob da istraži kod

blocks.en: Produces an implementation plan that the user approves
blocks.sr: Proizvodi plan implementacije koji korisnik odobrava

blocks.en: Safe exploration with no side effects
blocks.sr: Bezbedno istraživanje bez sporednih efekata

blocks.en: **When to use planning mode:**
blocks.sr: **Kada koristiti planski režim:**

blocks.en: Large changes (dozens of files)
blocks.sr: Velike izmene (desetine fajlova)

blocks.en: Multiple plausible approaches (microservices: how to define boundaries?)
blocks.sr: Više pristupa koji dolaze u obzir (mikroservisi: kako definisati granice?)

blocks.en: Architectural decisions (which framework? what structure?)
blocks.sr: Arhitektonske odluke (koji framework? kakva struktura?)

blocks.en: Unfamiliar codebase (you must understand before changing)
blocks.sr: Nepoznat kod (moraš da razumeš pre nego što menjaš)

blocks.en: Library migrations affecting 45+ files
blocks.sr: Migracije biblioteka koje pogađaju 45+ fajlova

blocks.en: **When to use direct execution:**
blocks.sr: **Kada koristiti direktno izvršavanje:**

blocks.en: Single-file fixes with a clear stack trace
blocks.sr: Ispravke u jednom fajlu sa jasnim stack trace-om

blocks.en: Adding one validation check
blocks.sr: Dodavanje jedne provere validacije

blocks.en: Well-understood, unambiguous changes
blocks.sr: Dobro shvaćene, nedvosmislene izmene

blocks.en: **Combined approach:**
blocks.sr: **Kombinovani pristup:**

blocks.en: Planning mode for investigation and design
blocks.sr: Planski režim za istraživanje i dizajn

blocks.en: User approves the plan
blocks.sr: Korisnik odobrava plan

blocks.en: Direct execution to implement the approved plan
blocks.sr: Direktno izvršavanje radi implementacije odobrenog plana

blocks.en: **Explore subagent** — a specialized subagent for exploring the codebase:
blocks.sr: **Explore podagent** — specijalizovan podagent za istraživanje koda:

blocks.en: Isolates verbose output from the main context
blocks.sr: Izoluje opširan izlaz od glavnog konteksta

blocks.en: Returns only a summary
blocks.sr: Vraća samo rezime

blocks.en: Prevents context-window exhaustion in multi-phase tasks
blocks.sr: Sprečava iscrpljivanje prozora konteksta u višefaznim zadacima

blocks.en: 5.7 The `/compact` Command
blocks.sr: 5.7 Komanda `/compact`

blocks.en: `/compact` is a built-in command for compressing context:
blocks.sr: `/compact` je ugrađena komanda za kompresiju konteksta:

blocks.en: Summarizes prior history to free up the context window
blocks.sr: Sumira prethodnu istoriju kako bi oslobodila prozor konteksta

blocks.en: Used in long investigation sessions when the context fills up with verbose tool output
blocks.sr: Koristi se u dugim istraživačkim sesijama kada se kontekst napuni opširnim izlazom alata

blocks.en: Risk: exact numeric values, dates, and specific details can be lost during summarization
blocks.sr: Rizik: tačne numeričke vrednosti, datumi i specifični detalji mogu se izgubiti tokom sumiranja

blocks.en: 5.8 The `/memory` Command
blocks.sr: 5.8 Komanda `/memory`

blocks.en: `/memory` is a built-in command for managing memory between sessions:
blocks.sr: `/memory` je ugrađena komanda za upravljanje memorijom između sesija:

blocks.en: Opens the `CLAUDE.md` file for editing, allowing you to save notes, preferences, and context
blocks.sr: Otvara fajl `CLAUDE.md` za izmenu, što omogućava da sačuvaš beleške, preferencije i kontekst

blocks.en: Information persists across sessions and is automatically loaded on startup
blocks.sr: Informacije se zadržavaju kroz sesije i automatski se učitavaju pri pokretanju

blocks.en: Useful for storing project conventions, user preferences, frequently used commands, and current work context
blocks.sr: Korisno za čuvanje projektnih konvencija, korisničkih preferencija, često korišćenih komandi i trenutnog konteksta rada

blocks.en: Alternative to re-explaining the same instructions in every session
blocks.sr: Alternativa ponovnom objašnjavanju istih instrukcija u svakoj sesiji

blocks.en: 5.9 Claude Code CLI for CI/CD
blocks.sr: 5.9 Claude Code CLI za CI/CD

blocks.en: **The `-p` (or `--print`) flag:**
blocks.sr: **Zastavica `-p` (ili `--print`):**

blocks.text: claude -p "Analyze this pull request for security issues"

blocks.en: Non-interactive mode: processes the prompt, prints to stdout, exits
blocks.sr: Neinteraktivni režim: obradi prompt, ispiše na stdout, izađe

blocks.en: Does not wait for user input
blocks.sr: Ne čeka korisnički unos

blocks.en: The only correct way to run Claude in CI/CD pipelines
blocks.sr: Jedini ispravan način da se Claude pokrene u CI/CD pipeline-ovima

blocks.en: **Structured output for CI:**
blocks.sr: **Strukturirani izlaz za CI:**

blocks.text: claude -p "Review this PR" --output-format json --json-schema '{"type":"object",...}'

blocks.en: `--output-format json` — output in JSON
blocks.sr: `--output-format json` — izlaz u JSON formatu

blocks.en: `--json-schema` — validate output against a schema
blocks.sr: `--json-schema` — validacija izlaza u odnosu na šemu

blocks.en: The result can be parsed to automatically post inline PR comments
blocks.sr: Rezultat se može parsirati kako bi se automatski postavili inline komentari na PR

blocks.en:
**Session context isolation:**
The same Claude session that generated code is often less effective at reviewing it (the model retains its reasoning context and is less likely to challenge its own decisions). Use an independent instance for review.
blocks.sr:
**Izolacija konteksta sesije:**
Ista Claude sesija koja je generisala kod često je manje efikasna u njegovom pregledu (model zadržava kontekst svog rezonovanja i manje je sklon da preispita sopstvene odluke). Za pregled koristi nezavisnu instancu.

blocks.en:
**Preventing duplicate comments:**
When re-reviewing after new commits, include prior review results in context and instruct Claude to report only new or unresolved issues.
blocks.sr:
**Sprečavanje dupliranih komentara:**
Kada radiš ponovni pregled posle novih commit-ova, uključi rezultate prethodnog pregleda u kontekst i uputi Claude da prijavi samo nove ili nerešene probleme.

blocks.en: 5.10 `fork_session` and Session Management
blocks.sr: 5.10 `fork_session` i upravljanje sesijama

blocks.en: **`--resume <session-name>`** resumes a named session:
blocks.sr: **`--resume <session-name>`** nastavlja imenovanu sesiju:

blocks.text: claude --resume investigation-auth-bug

blocks.en: Continues a prior conversation with saved context
blocks.sr: Nastavlja prethodni razgovor sa sačuvanim kontekstom

blocks.en: Useful for long investigations across multiple sessions
blocks.sr: Korisno za duga istraživanja koja se protežu kroz više sesija

blocks.en: Risk: if files changed since the prior session, tool results may be stale
blocks.sr: Rizik: ako su se fajlovi promenili od prethodne sesije, rezultati alata mogu biti zastareli

blocks.en: **`fork_session`** creates an independent branch from shared context:
blocks.sr: **`fork_session`** kreira nezavisnu granu iz deljenog konteksta:

blocks.text:
Codebase investigation
         |
    fork_session
    /           \
Approach A:      Approach B:
Redux            Context API

blocks.en: Both forks inherit context up to the branch point
blocks.sr: Obe grane nasleđuju kontekst do tačke grananja

blocks.en: Afterwards, they diverge independently
blocks.sr: Nakon toga se razilaze nezavisno

blocks.en: Useful for comparing approaches or testing strategies
blocks.sr: Korisno za poređenje pristupa ili testiranje strategija

blocks.en: **When to start a new session instead of resuming:**
blocks.sr: **Kada započeti novu sesiju umesto nastavljanja:**

blocks.en: Tool results are stale (files changed)
blocks.sr: Rezultati alata su zastareli (fajlovi su se promenili)

blocks.en: A lot of time has passed and context has degraded
blocks.sr: Prošlo je mnogo vremena i kontekst je degradirao

blocks.en: It is better to restart with "Here is a short summary of what we found: ..." than to resume with old tool data
blocks.sr: Bolje je krenuti iznova sa „Evo kratkog rezimea onoga što smo pronašli: ..." nego nastaviti sa starim podacima alata

blocks.en: Chapter 6: Prompt Engineering — Advanced Techniques
blocks.sr: Poglavlje 6: Inženjering promptova — napredne tehnike

blocks.en: Documentation: [Prompt Engineering](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/overview) | [Anthropic Cookbook](https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-cookbook)
blocks.sr: Dokumentacija: [Prompt Engineering](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/overview) | [Anthropic Cookbook](https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-cookbook)

blocks.en: 6.1 Few-shot Prompting
blocks.sr: 6.1 Few-shot prompting

blocks.en: Few-shot prompting is the inclusion of 2–4 input/output examples in a prompt to demonstrate the expected behavior.
blocks.sr: Few-shot prompting je uključivanje 2–4 primera ulaza/izlaza u prompt radi demonstracije očekivanog ponašanja.

blocks.en: **Why few-shot is more effective than textual descriptions:**
blocks.sr: **Zašto je few-shot efikasniji od tekstualnih opisa:**

blocks.en: A vague instruction like “be more precise” can be interpreted in many ways
blocks.sr: Nejasna instrukcija poput „budi precizniji“ može se tumačiti na mnogo načina

blocks.en: An example unambiguously shows the expected format and decision logic
blocks.sr: Primer nedvosmisleno pokazuje očekivani format i logiku odlučivanja

blocks.en: The model generalizes the pattern to new cases (it does not just repeat the examples)
blocks.sr: Model generalizuje obrazac na nove slučajeve (ne ponavlja prosto primere)

blocks.en: **Types of few-shot examples and when to use them:**
blocks.sr: **Tipovi few-shot primera i kada ih koristiti:**

blocks.en: **Examples for ambiguous scenarios:**
blocks.sr: **Primeri za dvosmislene scenarije:**

blocks.text:
Request: "My order is broken"
Action: Call get_customer -> lookup_order -> check status.
Rationale: “broken” may mean a damaged item; you need order details.

Request: "Get me a manager"
Action: Immediately call escalate_to_human.
Rationale: The customer explicitly requests a human. Do not attempt to solve autonomously.

blocks.en: **Examples for output formatting:**
blocks.sr: **Primeri za formatiranje izlaza:**

blocks.text:
Finding example:
{
  "location": "src/auth/login.ts:42",
  "issue": "SQL injection in the username parameter",
  "severity": "critical",
  "suggested_fix": "Use a parameterized query"
}

blocks.en: **Examples to separate acceptable vs problematic code:**
blocks.sr: **Primeri za razdvajanje prihvatljivog od problematičnog koda:**

blocks.en: **Examples for extraction from different document formats:**
blocks.sr: **Primeri za ekstrakciju iz različitih formata dokumenata:**

blocks.en: **Examples for informal measurements:**
blocks.sr: **Primeri za neformalne mere:**

blocks.en: Few-shot is especially effective for extracting informal and non-standard measurement units that are too diverse for purely rule-based instructions.
blocks.sr: Few-shot je posebno efikasan za ekstrakciju neformalnih i nestandardnih mernih jedinica koje su previše raznolike za instrukcije zasnovane isključivo na pravilima.

blocks.en: **Format normalization rules in prompts:**
blocks.sr: **Pravila normalizacije formata u promptovima:**

blocks.en: When using strict JSON schemas for structured output, add normalization rules in the prompt:
blocks.sr: Kada koristiš stroge JSON šeme za strukturirani izlaz, dodaj pravila normalizacije u prompt:

blocks.text:
Normalization:
- Dates: always ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD); "yesterday" -> compute an absolute date
- Currency: numeric amount + currency code; "five bucks" -> {"amount": 5, "currency": "USD"}
- Percentages: decimal fraction; "half" -> 0.5

blocks.en: This prevents semantic errors where the JSON is syntactically valid but values are inconsistent.
blocks.sr: Ovo sprečava semantičke greške gde je JSON sintaksno validan ali su vrednosti nedosledne.

blocks.en: 6.2 Explicit Criteria vs Vague Instructions
blocks.sr: 6.2 Eksplicitni kriterijumi naspram nejasnih instrukcija

blocks.en: **Bad (vague):**
blocks.sr: **Loše (nejasno):**

blocks.text:
Check code comments for accuracy.
Be conservative—report only high-confidence findings.

blocks.en: **Good (explicit criteria):**
blocks.sr: **Dobro (eksplicitni kriterijumi):**

blocks.text:
Flag a comment as problematic ONLY if:
1. The comment describes behavior that CONTRADICTS the actual code behavior
2. The comment references a non-existent function or variable
3. A TODO/FIXME comment refers to a bug that has already been fixed in code

Do NOT flag:
- Comments that are merely stylistically outdated
- Comments with minor wording inaccuracies
- Missing comments (that is a separate category)

blocks.en: **Define severity criteria with examples:**
blocks.sr: **Definiši kriterijume ozbiljnosti uz primere:**

blocks.text:
CRITICAL: Runtime failure for users
  Example: NullPointerException while processing a payment

HIGH: Security vulnerability
  Example: SQL injection, XSS, missing authorization checks

MEDIUM: Logic bug without immediate impact
  Example: Wrong sorting, off-by-one error

LOW: Code quality
  Example: Duplication, suboptimal algorithm for small data

blocks.en: 6.3 Prompt Chaining
blocks.sr: 6.3 Prompt chaining (ulančavanje promptova)

blocks.en: Prompt chaining breaks a complex task into a sequence of focused steps:
blocks.sr: Prompt chaining razlaže složen zadatak na niz fokusiranih koraka:

blocks.text:
Step 1: Analyze auth.ts (local issues only)
       -> Output: list of issues in auth.ts

Step 2: Analyze database.ts (local issues only)
       -> Output: list of issues in database.ts

Step 3: Integration pass (cross-file dependencies)
       -> Output: issues at module boundaries

blocks.en: **Why this matters:**
blocks.sr: **Zašto je ovo važno:**

blocks.en: Avoids **attention dilution**—when the model receives too many files at once, it may miss bugs in some files while providing shallow commentary on others
blocks.sr: Izbegava **razblaživanje pažnje** (attention dilution) — kada model primi previše fajlova odjednom, može da propusti bagove u nekim fajlovima, dok o drugima daje površne komentare

blocks.en: Ensures consistent analysis quality per file
blocks.sr: Obezbeđuje dosledan kvalitet analize po fajlu

blocks.en: Allows separate analysis of cross-file interactions
blocks.sr: Omogućava odvojenu analizu interakcija između fajlova

blocks.en: **When to use prompt chaining vs dynamic decomposition:**
blocks.sr: **Kada koristiti prompt chaining naspram dinamičke dekompozicije:**

blocks.en: **Prompt chaining** — predictable, repeatable tasks (code review, file migrations)
blocks.sr: **Prompt chaining** — predvidivi, ponovljivi zadaci (code review, migracije fajlova)

blocks.en: **Dynamic decomposition** — open-ended investigations where subtasks become clear only during execution
blocks.sr: **Dinamička dekompozicija** — otvorena istraživanja gde podzadaci postaju jasni tek tokom izvršavanja

blocks.en: 6.4 The “Interview” Pattern
blocks.sr: 6.4 Obrazac „intervju“

blocks.en: Before implementing a solution, Claude asks clarifying questions:
blocks.sr: Pre implementacije rešenja, Claude postavlja pitanja za pojašnjenje:

blocks.text:
Claude: "Before implementing caching for the API, a few questions:
1. Which cache invalidation strategy do you prefer—TTL or event-based?
2. Is stale data acceptable when the cache is unavailable?
3. Should caching be per-user or global?
4. What is the expected data volume to cache?"

blocks.en: **When this is useful:**
blocks.sr: **Kada je ovo korisno:**

blocks.en: Unfamiliar domain (fintech, healthcare, legal systems)
blocks.sr: Nepoznat domen (fintech, zdravstvo, pravni sistemi)

blocks.en: Tasks with non-obvious implications (cache strategies, failure modes)
blocks.sr: Zadaci sa neočiglednim posledicama (strategije keširanja, načini otkazivanja)

blocks.en: Multiple viable approaches where the best choice depends on context
blocks.sr: Više održivih pristupa gde najbolji izbor zavisi od konteksta

blocks.en: 6.5 Validation and Retry-with-Feedback
blocks.sr: 6.5 Validacija i retry-with-feedback (ponovni pokušaj uz povratnu informaciju)

blocks.en: When extracted data fails validation:
blocks.sr: Kada ekstrahovani podaci ne prođu validaciju:

blocks.text:
Step 1: Extract data from the document
Step 2: Validate (Pydantic, JSON Schema, business rules)
Step 3: If there's an error—retry with context:
  - The original document
  - The previous (incorrect) extraction
  - The specific error: "Field 'total' = 150, but sum(line_items) = 145. Re-check values."

blocks.en: **When retry will be effective:**
blocks.sr: **Kada će ponovni pokušaj biti efikasan:**

blocks.en: Format errors (date in the wrong format)
blocks.sr: Greške formata (datum u pogrešnom formatu)

blocks.en: Structural errors (a field placed in the wrong location)
blocks.sr: Strukturne greške (polje postavljeno na pogrešno mesto)

blocks.en: Arithmetic inconsistencies (the model can re-check)
blocks.sr: Aritmetičke nedoslednosti (model može ponovo da proveri)

blocks.en: **When retry will NOT help:**
blocks.sr: **Kada ponovni pokušaj NEĆE pomoći:**

blocks.en: The information is absent from the source document
blocks.sr: Informacija ne postoji u izvornom dokumentu

blocks.en: The required context is external (the data is in another document not provided)
blocks.sr: Potreban kontekst je eksterni (podatak je u drugom dokumentu koji nije priložen)

blocks.en: **Pydantic as a validation tool:**
blocks.sr: **Pydantic kao alat za validaciju:**

blocks.en: Pydantic is a Python library for schema-based data validation. For the exam, the key points are:
blocks.sr: Pydantic je Python biblioteka za validaciju podataka po šemi. Za ispit je ključno sledeće:

blocks.en: **Structural validation:** types, requiredness, enum constraints checked in code after receiving JSON from Claude
blocks.sr: **Strukturna validacija:** tipovi, obaveznost i enum ograničenja proveravaju se u kodu nakon prijema JSON-a od Claude-a

blocks.en: **Semantic validation:** custom validators enforce business logic (sum of items equals total; start_date < end_date)
blocks.sr: **Semantička validacija:** prilagođeni validatori sprovode poslovnu logiku (zbir stavki jednak ukupnom iznosu; start_date < end_date)

blocks.en: **Validate–retry loops:** on Pydantic validation failure, construct an error message and re-prompt Claude with the error context
blocks.sr: **Petlje validacije i ponovnog pokušaja:** kada Pydantic validacija ne uspe, sastavi poruku o grešci i ponovo pošalji prompt Claude-u sa kontekstom greške

blocks.en: **JSON Schema generation:** Pydantic models can generate JSON Schema for `tool_use`, providing a single source of truth
blocks.sr: **Generisanje JSON šeme:** Pydantic modeli mogu da generišu JSON šemu za `tool_use`, pružajući jedinstveni izvor istine

blocks.en: 6.6 Self-correction
blocks.sr: 6.6 Samoispravljanje (self-correction)

blocks.en: A pattern for detecting internal contradictions:
blocks.sr: Obrazac za otkrivanje internih protivrečnosti:

blocks.en: The model extracts both the stated value and a computed value—if they differ, `conflict_detected` allows you to handle the discrepancy.
blocks.sr: Model ekstrahuje i navedenu i izračunatu vrednost — ako se razlikuju, `conflict_detected` ti omogućava da obradiš neslaganje.

blocks.en: Chapter 7: Message Batches API
blocks.sr: Poglavlje 7: Message Batches API

blocks.en: Documentation: [Message Batches](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/message-batches)
blocks.sr: Dokumentacija: [Message Batches](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/message-batches)

blocks.en: 7.1 Overview
blocks.sr: 7.1 Pregled

blocks.en: The Message Batches API lets you submit batches of requests for asynchronous processing:
blocks.sr: Message Batches API vam omogućava da pošaljete grupe zahteva na asinhronu obradu:

cells.en: Up to **24 hours** (no latency SLA guarantee)
cells.sr: Do **24 sata** (bez SLA garancije za latenciju)

cells.en: `custom_id` field to link request and response
cells.sr: polje `custom_id` za povezivanje zahteva i odgovora

blocks.en: 7.2 When to Use Batch API vs Synchronous API
blocks.sr: 7.2 Kada koristiti Batch API, a kada sinhroni API

cells.en: The developer is waiting; 24 hours is unacceptable
Result is needed by morning; 50% savings
cells.sr: Rezultat je potreban do jutra; ušteda 50%

blocks.en: 7.3 Using `custom_id`
blocks.sr: 7.3 Korišćenje `custom_id`

blocks.en: `custom_id` allows you to:
blocks.sr: `custom_id` vam omogućava da:

blocks.en: Link the result to the original document
blocks.sr: povežete rezultat sa originalnim dokumentom

blocks.en: On failure, re-submit only the failed documents
blocks.sr: u slučaju neuspeha, ponovo pošaljete samo dokumente koji nisu uspeli

blocks.en: Avoid re-processing successful documents
blocks.sr: izbegnete ponovnu obradu uspešnih dokumenata

blocks.en: 7.4 Handling Failures in Batches
blocks.sr: 7.4 Rukovanje neuspesima u grupama

blocks.en: Submit a batch of 100 documents
blocks.sr: Pošaljete grupu od 100 dokumenata

blocks.en: 95 succeed; 5 fail (context limit exceeded)
blocks.sr: 95 uspeva; 5 ne uspeva (prekoračen limit konteksta)

blocks.en: Identify failures by `custom_id`
blocks.sr: Identifikujete neuspehe pomoću `custom_id`

blocks.en: Modify strategy (e.g., split long documents into chunks)
blocks.sr: Izmenite strategiju (npr. podelite duge dokumente na delove)

blocks.en: Re-submit only the 5 failed documents
blocks.sr: Ponovo pošaljete samo 5 dokumenata koji nisu uspeli

blocks.en: 7.5 SLA Planning
blocks.sr: 7.5 Planiranje SLA

blocks.en: If you need a result in 30 hours and the Batch API can take up to 24 hours:
blocks.sr: Ako vam je rezultat potreban za 30 sati, a obrada kroz Batch API može da traje do 24 sata:

blocks.en: Submission window: 30 - 24 = **6 hours**
blocks.sr: Prozor za slanje: 30 - 24 = **6 sati**

blocks.en: Batches must be submitted no later than 24 hours before the deadline
blocks.sr: Grupe se moraju poslati najkasnije 24 sata pre roka

blocks.en: For frequent submissions, split into 4-hour windows
blocks.sr: Za česta slanja, podelite na prozore od 4 sata

blocks.en: 8.1 Fixed Pipelines (Prompt Chaining)
blocks.sr: 8.1 Fiksni pipeline-ovi (Prompt Chaining)

blocks.en: Each step is defined in advance:
blocks.sr: Svaki korak je definisan unapred:

blocks.text: Document -> Metadata extraction -> Data extraction -> Validation -> Enrichment -> Final output

blocks.en: **When to use:**
blocks.sr: **Kada koristiti:**

blocks.en: The task structure is predictable (reviews always follow the same template)
blocks.sr: Struktura zadatka je predvidljiva (recenzije uvek prate isti šablon)

blocks.en: All steps are known up front
blocks.sr: Svi koraci su poznati unapred

blocks.en: You need stability and reproducibility
blocks.sr: Potrebne su vam stabilnost i ponovljivost

blocks.en: 8.2 Dynamic Adaptive Decomposition
blocks.sr: 8.2 Dinamička adaptivna dekompozicija

blocks.en: Subtasks are generated based on intermediate results:
blocks.sr: Podzadaci se generišu na osnovu međurezultata:

blocks.text:
1. "Add tests for a legacy codebase"
2. -> First: map the structure (Glob, Grep)
3. -> Found: 3 modules with no tests, 2 with partial coverage
4. -> Prioritize: start with the payments module (high risk)
5. -> During work: discovered a dependency on an external API
6. -> Adapt: add a mock for the external API before writing tests

blocks.en: **When to use:**
blocks.sr: **Kada koristiti:**

blocks.en: Open-ended investigative tasks
blocks.sr: Otvoreni istraživački zadaci

blocks.en: When the full scope is unknown up front
blocks.sr: Kada pun obim nije poznat unapred

blocks.en: When each step depends on the results of the previous step
blocks.sr: Kada svaki korak zavisi od rezultata prethodnog koraka

blocks.en: 8.3 Multi-pass Code Review
blocks.sr: 8.3 Višeprolazni pregled koda (Multi-pass)

blocks.en: For pull requests with 10+ files:
blocks.sr: Za pull request-ove sa 10+ fajlova:

blocks.text:
Pass 1 (per-file): Analyze auth.ts -> list local issues
Pass 1 (per-file): Analyze database.ts -> list local issues
Pass 1 (per-file): Analyze routes.ts -> list local issues
...
Pass 2 (integration): Analyze relationships between files
  -> Cross-file issues: inconsistent types, circular dependencies

blocks.en: **Why a single pass over 14 files is bad:**
blocks.sr: **Zašto je jedan prolaz preko 14 fajlova loš:**

blocks.en: Attention dilution: deep analysis for some files, shallow for others
blocks.sr: Razblaživanje pažnje: duboka analiza za neke fajlove, površna za druge

blocks.en: Inconsistent comments: a pattern is flagged in one file but approved in another
blocks.sr: Nedosledni komentari: obrazac se označi kao problem u jednom fajlu, a odobri u drugom

blocks.en: Missed bugs: obvious errors get skipped due to cognitive overload
blocks.sr: Propušteni bagovi: očigledne greške se preskaču zbog kognitivnog preopterećenja

blocks.en: Chapter 9: Escalation and Human-in-the-Loop
blocks.sr: Poglavlje 9: Eskalacija i čovek u petlji (human-in-the-loop)

blocks.en: 9.1 When to Escalate to a Human
blocks.sr: 9.1 Kada eskalirati ka čoveku

blocks.en: **Escalation triggers (clear rules):**
blocks.sr: **Okidači za eskalaciju (jasna pravila):**

cells.en: The customer explicitly asks “get me a manager”
Escalate immediately; do not attempt to solve
cells.sr: Eskaliraj odmah; ne pokušavaj da rešiš

cells.en: Policy does not cover the request
Escalate (e.g., competitor price matching when policy is silent)
cells.sr: Eskaliraj (npr. izjednačavanje cene sa konkurencijom kada politika o tome ćuti)

cells.en: Escalate after a reasonable number of attempts
Escalate (preferably enforced via a hook, not a prompt)
cells.sr: Eskaliraj (po mogućstvu sprovedeno preko hook-a, a ne prompta)

cells.en: Multiple matches when searching for a customer
Ask for additional identifiers; do not guess

blocks.en: **What is NOT a reliable trigger:**
blocks.sr: **Šta NIJE pouzdan okidač:**

cells.en: Customer mood does not correlate with case complexity
cells.sr: Raspoloženje klijenta ne korelira sa složenošću slučaja

cells.en: The model can be confidently wrong; calibration is poor
cells.sr: Model može samouvereno da greši; kalibracija je loša

cells.en: Overengineering; may require training data you don’t have
cells.sr: Prekomplikovano (overengineering); može zahtevati podatke za obuku koje nemaš

blocks.en: 9.2 Escalation Patterns
blocks.sr: 9.2 Obrasci eskalacije

blocks.en: **Immediate escalation:**
blocks.sr: **Trenutna eskalacija:**

blocks.text:
Customer: "I want to speak to a manager"
Agent: [immediately calls escalate_to_human]
NOT: "I can help with your issue, let me..."

blocks.en: **Escalation after an attempt to resolve:**
blocks.sr: **Eskalacija posle pokušaja rešavanja:**

blocks.text:
Customer: "My refrigerator broke two days after purchase"
Agent: [checks the order, offers a warranty replacement]
If the customer is not satisfied -> escalate

blocks.en: **Nuanced escalation (acknowledge → resolve → escalate on reiteration):**
blocks.sr: **Nijansirana eskalacija (priznaj → reši → eskaliraj na ponovljeni zahtev):**

blocks.text:
Customer: "This is outrageous, I'm very unhappy with the quality!"
Agent: [acknowledges frustration] "I understand your frustration."
       [offers resolution] "I can offer a replacement or a refund."
Customer: "No, I want to talk to someone!"
Agent: [customer insists again -> immediate escalation]

blocks.en: Key principle: acknowledge emotion first, then propose a concrete solution, and only escalate if the customer reiterates the desire for a human. Do not escalate on the first expression of dissatisfaction (that is not the same as requesting a manager).
blocks.sr: Ključni princip: prvo priznaj emociju, zatim predloži konkretno rešenje, i eskaliraj tek ako klijent ponovi želju za čovekom. Ne eskaliraj na prvi izraz nezadovoljstva (to nije isto što i traženje menadžera).

blocks.en: **Escalation for a policy gap:**
blocks.sr: **Eskalacija zbog rupe u politici:**

blocks.text:
Customer: "Competitor X has this item 30% cheaper—give me a discount"
Policy: covers price adjustments only on your own site
Agent: [escalates — policy does not cover competitor price matching]

blocks.en: 9.3 Structured Handoff Protocols
blocks.sr: 9.3 Protokoli strukturirane primopredaje (handoff)

blocks.en: On escalation, the agent should pass a structured summary to a human:
blocks.sr: Pri eskalaciji, agent treba da prosledi strukturiran rezime čoveku:

blocks.text:
{
  "customer_id": "CUST-12345",
  "customer_name": "Ivan Petrov",
  "issue_summary": "Refund request for a damaged item",
  "order_id": "ORD-67890",
  "root_cause": "Item arrived damaged; photos attached",
  "actions_taken": [
    "Verified customer via get_customer",
    "Confirmed order via lookup_order",
    "Offered a standard replacement — customer insists on a refund"
  ],
  "refund_amount": "$89.99",
  "recommended_action": "Approve a full refund",
  "escalation_reason": "Customer requested to speak with a manager"
}

blocks.en: The human operator does not have access to the full conversation transcript—they only see this summary. Therefore it must be complete and self-contained.
blocks.sr: Ljudski operater nema pristup celokupnom transkriptu razgovora — vidi samo ovaj rezime. Zato on mora biti potpun i samodovoljan.

blocks.en: 9.4 Confidence Calibration and Human Oversight
blocks.sr: 9.4 Kalibracija pouzdanosti i ljudski nadzor

blocks.en: For data extraction systems:
blocks.sr: Za sisteme za ekstrakciju podataka:

blocks.en: **Field-level confidence scores:** the model outputs a confidence score per extracted field
blocks.sr: **Skorovi pouzdanosti na nivou polja:** model daje skor pouzdanosti za svako ekstrahovano polje

blocks.en: **Calibration:** use labeled validation sets to tune thresholds
blocks.sr: **Kalibracija:** koristi označene validacione skupove za podešavanje pragova

blocks.en: **Routing:**
blocks.sr: **Rutiranje:**

blocks.en: High confidence + stable accuracy -> automated processing
blocks.sr: Visoka pouzdanost + stabilna tačnost -> automatska obrada

blocks.en: Low confidence or ambiguous sources -> human review
blocks.sr: Niska pouzdanost ili dvosmisleni izvori -> ljudska provera

blocks.en: **Stratified random sampling:**
blocks.sr: **Stratifikovano slučajno uzorkovanje:**

blocks.en: Even for high-confidence extractions, regularly audit a sample
blocks.sr: Čak i za ekstrakcije sa visokom pouzdanošću, redovno proveravaj uzorak

blocks.en: An aggregate 97% accuracy can hide 40% errors for a particular document type
blocks.sr: Agregatna tačnost od 97% može da sakrije 40% grešaka za određeni tip dokumenta

blocks.en: Analyze accuracy by document type and by field, not only overall
blocks.sr: Analiziraj tačnost po tipu dokumenta i po polju, a ne samo ukupno

blocks.en: Chapter 10: Error Handling in Multi-agent Systems
blocks.sr: Poglavlje 10: Rukovanje greškama u multi-agentskim sistemima

blocks.en: 10.1 Error Categories
blocks.sr: 10.1 Kategorije grešaka

cells.en: Invalid input format, missing required field
cells.sr: Nevalidan format ulaza, nedostaje obavezno polje

cells.en: Explain to the user; propose an alternative

blocks.en: 10.2 Error-handling Anti-patterns
blocks.sr: 10.2 Anti-obrasci u rukovanju greškama

cells.en: The coordinator can’t decide how to recover
cells.sr: Koordinator ne može da odluči kako da se oporavi

cells.en: Return error type, query, partial results, alternatives
cells.sr: Vrati tip greške, upit, delimične rezultate, alternative

cells.en: Coordinator thinks there were no matches, but it was a failure
cells.sr: Koordinator misli da nije bilo poklapanja, a zapravo je bio otkaz

cells.en: Aborting the whole workflow on one failure
cells.sr: Prekidanje celog radnog toka zbog jednog otkaza

cells.en: Continue with partial results; annotate gaps
cells.sr: Nastavi sa delimičnim rezultatima; označi praznine

cells.en: Local recovery (1–2 retries), then propagate to coordinator
cells.sr: Lokalni oporavak (1–2 pokušaja), zatim propagiraj koordinatoru

blocks.en: 10.3 A Structured Subagent Error
blocks.sr: 10.3 Strukturisana greška podagenta

blocks.en: This provides the coordinator with the information needed to decide:
blocks.sr: Ovo koordinatoru pruža informacije potrebne da odluči:

blocks.en: Retry with a modified query?
blocks.sr: Ponoviti sa izmenjenim upitom?

blocks.en: Use partial results?
blocks.sr: Iskoristiti delimične rezultate?

blocks.en: Delegate to a different subagent?
blocks.sr: Delegirati drugom podagentu?

blocks.en: Continue without this section and annotate the gap?
blocks.sr: Nastaviti bez ovog odeljka i označiti prazninu?

blocks.en: 10.4 Coverage Annotations in the Final Synthesis
blocks.sr: 10.4 Anotacije pokrivenosti u finalnoj sintezi

blocks.text:
## Report: AI Impact on Creative Industries

### Visual Art (FULL COVERAGE)
[research results]

### Music (PARTIAL COVERAGE — search agent timeout)
[partial results]
⚠️ Note: coverage for this section is limited due to a timeout in the search agent.

### Literature (FULL COVERAGE)
[research results]

blocks.en: 11.1 Extract Facts into a Separate Block
